finding_results.notes: Replaced Mallick's single composed mini-speech with two separate conversational moves. Move 1 (line 235): Mallick observes Joel's calm and attributes it to his profession; Joel responds with a minimal 'OK' (line 237). Move 2 (line 239): Mallick raises the argument and the clean account as separate observations, ending with 'You don't have any of that' rather than a binary courtroom formulation. The either-or summation is eliminated. Mallick stays in her folksy register throughout, using contractions and hedging ('I don't mean that in a bad way,' 'I'm not pushing on that,' 'Everybody else has got something out of place'). The pressure arrives through accumulation across the two moves, not through a single diagnostic speech. Pen choreography preserved: pen goes down at line 229, stays down through the exchange, picked up again at line 247 for the 'probably' landing.

summary: Single finding addressed: Mallick's composed pressure beat broken from one uninterrupted mini-speech into two conversational moves with Joel responding between them. The tidy binary conclusion removed; suspicion now lands through observational accumulation. Mallick's folksy register and high contraction level preserved. No collateral changes to surrounding text; heading contract, continuity, setups (mallick_corrections mechanism), and chapter state shift ('either an idiot or the most dangerous person') all intact.
